_meta.purpose: Urine microscopy (sediment) finding -> clinical association. Keys are YOLO class names (snake_case) from the urine-sediment detector. Resolves each element (casts / cells / crystals / organisms) to its significance + association. Decision support only; correlate with dipstick, pH and clinical picture.
_meta.source: Standard urinalysis references (Strasinger; CLSI GP16). Curated for JORINOVA NEXUS.

hyaline_cast.name: Hyaline cast
hyaline_cast.disease: Non-specific — dehydration, exercise, fever; few are normal
hyaline_cast.note: A few hyaline casts can be normal.

granular_cast.name: Granular cast
granular_cast.disease: Acute tubular necrosis, glomerulonephritis, nephron injury

rbc_cast.name: Red-cell cast
rbc_cast.note: Critical: highly specific for glomerular bleeding — urgent nephrology.

waxy_cast.name: Waxy cast
waxy_cast.disease: Chronic kidney disease / advanced renal failure
waxy_cast.note: Wide, brittle; sign of urine stasis in dilated tubules.

rbc.name: Red blood cell (urine)
rbc.disease: Haematuria — UTI, stones, glomerular disease, tumour, trauma
rbc.note: Dysmorphic RBCs suggest glomerular origin; isomorphic suggest lower tract.

wbc.name: White blood cell / pus cell (urine)
wbc.disease: UTI / inflammation; sterile pyuria (TB, interstitial nephritis)

squamous_epithelial.name: Squamous epithelial cell
squamous_epithelial.note: Many squamous cells -> poorly collected sample; consider recollect.

transitional_epithelial.name: Transitional (urothelial) epithelial cell
transitional_epithelial.disease: Lower urinary tract irritation; atypical forms -> urothelial carcinoma (cytology)

calcium_oxalate_crystal.name: Calcium oxalate crystal
calcium_oxalate_crystal.note: Envelope (dihydrate) common/benign; needle (monohydrate) -> consider ethylene glycol.

uric_acid_crystal.name: Uric acid crystal
uric_acid_crystal.disease: Gout, tumour lysis syndrome, acidic urine

triple_phosphate_crystal.name: Triple phosphate (struvite) crystal
triple_phosphate_crystal.disease: Alkaline urine; urease-producing UTI (Proteus) -> staghorn calculi

cystine_crystal.name: Cystine crystal
cystine_crystal.note: Hexagonal plates are ALWAYS abnormal — diagnostic of cystinuria.

bacteria.name: Bacteria (urine)
bacteria.note: Significant with WBCs + symptoms; interpret with culture.

trichomonas.name: Trichomonas vaginalis
trichomonas.note: Motile flagellate; often genital contamination of urine.

sperm.name: Spermatozoa
sperm.disease: Post-ejaculation / retrograde — usually not reported clinically

artifact.name: Artefact / contaminant
artifact.disease: Contaminant (starch, fibre, bubble) — not a true finding
